name: SWEET & SOUR CRISPY TOFU
description: Sweet and sour needs no introduction! The dish is a go-to lunch whenever classic things will hit the spot in the restaurants, but this version is loads cheaper than ordering and the way the crispy fried tofu contrasts with the sweet punchy sauce, for this lows are crisp can't beat it. plus it's naturally vegan so ideal for everyone.
method: Small saucepan on a low-medium heat | Tofu press or 2 clean tea towels and a weight such as a heavy book | 2 x large frying pans on a medium-high heat | Fine grater
Press the following 240g press or place between two clean tea towels, put a weight such as a heavy book on top and leave to drain for at least half an hour

Put the ginger by scraping off the skin with a spoon and then grate it. Peel and finely dice the onion. Peel and finely dice the garlic. Add the 30ml of oil to one of the frying pans and fry the onion until soft (about 3-4 minutes)

Put the pineapple juice into another pan. Put the sesame oil into the small saucepan and place over a medium-high heat. Add the 2 cloves away for about 2 minutes until you have a lovely spice. Then set aside to cool completely

Meanwhile, put the remaining 750ml of oil in the other large frying pan with the cornflour and mix together to make a slurry out the drained tofu. Dip chunks and add them to the soy milk, ensuring each piece gets a good coating and then into the slurry they're well coated

Heat the vegetable oil to one of the frying pans. Add the tofu pieces and fry for about 3-4 minutes until golden brown and formed a crispy coating, about 5-10 minutes (be very careful not to burn). Working so keep them apart. Take the pan off the heat

Meanwhile, heat the sesame oil in the second frying pan. Add the onion, ginger and stir and cook for about 3 minutes. Add the green pepper and continue to cook for another 2 minutes. Add the pineapple and stir for another 2-5 minutes, stirring all the time. Drain the pineapple and add to the pan, continuing to stir and cook for 1 minute more. Remove the frying pan and heat. Pour over the sweet and sour sauce and fold it around the vegetables so that everything is well covered and warmed through, about 1-2 minutes
